sourceMovie.title: Chaplin
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Chaplin (1992) as a drama theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: Darkest Hour
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Chaplin clicked for you, Darkest Hour (2017) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Donnie Brasco
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Chaplin clicked for you, Donnie Brasco (1997) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Chaplin?
faq.answer: Try Darkest Hour, Donnie Brasco, Public Enemies—each shares DNA with Chaplin while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Chaplin?
faq.answer: Chaplin (1992) is best described as Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Chaplin worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
